notes: In the opening scene, the main characters jump off the train. Barrett runs away and says 'Follow Me', that first fight crashes the game with the following pop up message: "Error An unknown exception has occurred."  -> Settings Tried that Didn't Work: Full screen and windowed (ff7 launcher settings menu), Disabled Esync globally (in Proton's user-settings.py for each proton version).  -> Proton Versions Tried that Didn't Work: 3.7-8, 3.7-8 Beta, 3.16-4, 3.16-6 Beta.
title: FINAL FANTASY VII

title: FINAL FANTASY VII

notes: midgdar completed and there is no issue,work flawlessly without tweaks
title: FINAL FANTASY VII

notes: Did a full play through of the game using a Bluetooth connected DS4 controller. The only issue I came across at all was less related to the game and more to do with the controller configuration: it seemed like the controller buttons which I had mapped to keypresses per a popular configuration option in Steam were also sending actual controller inputs *sometimes* to the game which meant it was impossible to use a mapping that would use the original button configuration (circle for OK and X for cancel) as the joystick mappings themselves would still happen and the behavior was weird. Other than that, I played the game start to finish and never experienced so much as a hiccup.
title: FINAL FANTASY VII

notes: Played 9 hours so far, no problems of any kind

notes:
Some tweaks (optional): If you have some graphical glitches at first Mako reactor, in Launcher options turn on Original Graphic mode. Or you may continue playing like that, those glitches were only in one area and it doesn't affect the gameplay, however I cannot guarantee that they won't be later in game.
What was tested: All Midgar section, Moving and Fighting in Encounters in Global Map.
Performance: Awesome
